title: STEINS;GATE
notes: Errors weren't found. Performance quite stable

title: STEINS;GATE
notes: You must to disable your joysticks launching the system32\control.exe manually using proton and their STEAM_COMPAT_DATA_PATH

title: STEINS;GATE
notes: Go to ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/412830/pfx and run "rm z:; ln -s ~ z:". Apparently the game tries to write to "/My Games" (which doesn't work since it is not root!), but is perfectly happy with "~".

title: STEINS;GATE
notes: works great, though wacom tablet has to be unplugged. This game misinterprets wacom's input.

I initially had to set the launch option "PROTON_USE_WINED3D11"; otherwise it would give an error saying something along the lines of "D3DXCompileShader Failed". However, now it seems to work fine when I remove that option. Apparently setting that option triggered the installation of DirectX, which the game needed somehow.
title: STEINS;GATE

notes:
Game played to about 80% completion. No issues at all. Runs as expected. Controller works as expected.

Two small annoyances:
- has a launcher that starts every time and minimizes BigPicture-Mode
- When controller disconnects you have to restart the game to get the controller input working again
title: STEINS;GATE

notes: !!QuickSave does not function until a Manual Save is performed. I tested this multiple times to confirm.  Game save writes to .../steamapps/compatdata/412830/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/mages_steam/STEINS;GATE/eng/SAVEDATA.DAT. I confirmed that the file was created on game install, but mtime would not update upon QuickSave and only updated on Manual Save. Once I performed a single Manual Save, QuickSave began to work as expected.
